Day 100: Day 100 Assessment - 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📝

Day 100

Assessment Day

The Velveteen Rabbit

Page 2

📝 Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • 📚 Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 96-100
  • 🎯 Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

💡 Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. cottage

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Page 4

🎯 Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

✏️ Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

💭 Writing Assessment

Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Requirements: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Page 7

🎉

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Velveteen Rabbit.

See you on Day 101!

Day 101: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 101

2nd Grade ELA

The Velveteen Rabbit

Chapter 11

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 101!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from The Velveteen Rabbit
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • 🎯 Write: Informative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

cottage

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
clever

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

💡 Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

📖 Reading: The Velveteen Rabbit

Chapter 11

[Reading passage from The Velveteen Rabbit, Chapter 11 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 11]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 11]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 11]

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 11]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 101! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Velveteen Rabbit.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 101!

You've completed Day 102! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Velveteen Rabbit.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 102!

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 103!

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 104!

Day 109: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Day 109

2nd Grade ELA

The Velveteen Rabbit

Chapter 19

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 109!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from The Velveteen Rabbit
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Action vs. Linking Verbs
  • 🎯 Write: Opinion Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Idioms and Figurative Language

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

treasure

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
journey

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

💡 Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

📖 Reading: The Velveteen Rabbit

Chapter 19

[Reading passage from The Velveteen Rabbit, Chapter 19 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 19]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 19]

Question 3:

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 19]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 109! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Velveteen Rabbit.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 109!

Day 110: Day 110 Assessment - 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📝

Day 110

Assessment Day

The Velveteen Rabbit

Page 2

📝 Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • 📚 Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 106-110
  • 🎯 Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Informative Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

💡 Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. cottage

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Page 4

🎯 Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

✏️ Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

📝 Writing Assessment

Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Requirements: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Page 7

🎉

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Velveteen Rabbit.

See you on Day 111!

  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • 🎯 Write: Narrative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

📚 Vocabulary Words

cottage

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

Chapter 21

[Reading passage from The Velveteen Rabbit, Chapter 21 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Question 1:

Question 2:

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 21]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 111! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Velveteen Rabbit.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 111!

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 112!

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 113!

Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 119! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Velveteen Rabbit.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 119!

Day 120: Day 120 Assessment - 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📝

Day 120

Assessment Day

The Velveteen Rabbit

Page 2

📝 Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • 📚 Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 116-120
  • 🎯 Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Narrative Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

💡 Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. cottage

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Page 4

🎯 Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

✏️ Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

📖 Writing Assessment

Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Requirements: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Page 7

🎉

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Velveteen Rabbit.

See you on Day 121!

Page 1

📚

Day 121

2nd Grade ELA

Winnie-the-Pooh

Chapter 1

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 121!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from Winnie-the-Pooh
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • 🎯 Write: Opinion Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

cottage

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
clever

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

💡 Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

📖 Reading: Winnie-the-Pooh

Chapter 1

[Reading passage from Winnie-the-Pooh, Chapter 1 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 1]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 1]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 1]

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 1]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 121! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 121!

You've completed Day 122! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 122!

Day 123: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Day 123

2nd Grade ELA

Winnie-the-Pooh

  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Compound Sentences
  • 🎯 Write: Narrative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Root Words and Affixes

brave

Chapter 3

Page 7

🎉

You've completed Day 123! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 123!

Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:

📖 Narrative Writing

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 129!

Day 130: Day 130 Assessment - 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📝

Day 130

Assessment Day

Winnie-the-Pooh

Page 2

📝 Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • 📚 Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 126-130
  • 🎯 Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

💡 Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. cottage

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Page 4

🎯 Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

✏️ Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

💭 Writing Assessment

Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Requirements: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Page 7

🎉

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of Winnie-the-Pooh.

See you on Day 131!

Day 131: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 131

2nd Grade ELA

Winnie-the-Pooh

Chapter 11

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 131!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from Winnie-the-Pooh
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • 🎯 Write: Informative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

cottage

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
clever

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

💡 Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

📖 Reading: Winnie-the-Pooh

Chapter 11

[Reading passage from Winnie-the-Pooh, Chapter 11 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 11]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 11]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 11]

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 11]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 131! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 131!

Day 132

Great Job!

You've completed Day 132! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 132!

Day 133: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Day 133

2nd Grade ELA

Winnie-the-Pooh

Today you will:

  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Compound Sentences
  • 🎯 Write: Opinion Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Root Words and Affixes

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

📚 Vocabulary Words

brave

Chapter 13

[Reading passage from Winnie-the-Pooh, Chapter 13 will be inserted here from digital book database]

You've completed Day 133! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 133!

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 134!

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Page 4

🎯 Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

✏️ Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

📝 Writing Assessment

Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Requirements: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Page 7

🎉

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of Winnie-the-Pooh.

See you on Day 141!

Day 141: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 141

2nd Grade ELA

Winnie-the-Pooh

Chapter 21

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 141!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from Winnie-the-Pooh
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • 🎯 Write: Narrative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

cottage

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
clever

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

💡 Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

📖 Reading: Winnie-the-Pooh

Chapter 21

[Reading passage from Winnie-the-Pooh, Chapter 21 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 21]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 21]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 21]

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 21]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 141! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 141!

Day 142: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 142

2nd Grade ELA

Winnie-the-Pooh

Today you will:

  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Prepositional Phrases
  • 🎯 Write: Opinion Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Context Clues

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

📚 Vocabulary Words

forest

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

Chapter 22

[Reading passage from Winnie-the-Pooh, Chapter 22 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 22]

Question 2:

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 142! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 142!

Day 143: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 143

2nd Grade ELA

Winnie-the-Pooh

📖 Welcome to Day 143!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from Winnie-the-Pooh
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Compound Sentences
  • 🎯 Write: Informative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Root Words and Affixes

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

📚 Vocabulary Words

brave

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

Chapter 23

You've completed Day 143! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Winnie-the-Pooh.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 143!

Day 144: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

📚

Day 144

2nd Grade ELA

  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Action vs. Linking Verbs

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 144!

Day 145: Day 145 Assessment - 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📝

Day 145

Assessment Day

Winnie-the-Pooh

Page 2

📝 Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • 📚 Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 141-145
  • 🎯 Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

💡 Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. cottage

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Requirements: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

Day 151: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 151

2nd Grade ELA

The Little Prince

Chapter 1

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 151!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from The Little Prince
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • 🎯 Write: Opinion Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

cottage

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
clever

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

💡 Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

📖 Reading: The Little Prince

Chapter 1

[Reading passage from The Little Prince, Chapter 1 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 1]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 1]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 1]

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 1]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 151! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 151!

Day 152

The Little Prince

Today you will:

  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Prepositional Phrases
  • 🎯 Write: Informative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Context Clues

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

forest

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

📖 Reading: The Little Prince

Chapter 2

[Reading passage from The Little Prince, Chapter 2 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Question 2:

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 152! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 152!

Day 153: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 153

2nd Grade ELA

The Little Prince

Chapter 3

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 153!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from The Little Prince
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Compound Sentences
  • 🎯 Write: Narrative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Root Words and Affixes

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

brave

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

[Reading passage from The Little Prince, Chapter 3 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

Question 1:

Page 7

Great Job!

You've completed Day 153! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 153!

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 154!

Day 155: Day 155 Assessment - 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📝

Day 155

Assessment Day

The Little Prince

Page 2

📝 Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • 📚 Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 151-155
  • 🎯 Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Informative Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

💡 Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. cottage

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Page 4

🎯 Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

✏️ Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

📝 Writing Assessment

Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Requirements: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Page 7

🎉

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Little Prince.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 161!

Day 162: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 162

2nd Grade ELA

The Little Prince

Chapter 12

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 162!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from The Little Prince
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Prepositional Phrases
  • 🎯 Write: Narrative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Context Clues

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

forest

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
wicked

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

💡 Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

📖 Reading: The Little Prince

Chapter 12

[Reading passage from The Little Prince, Chapter 12 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 12]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 12]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 12]

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 12]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 162! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 162!

Day 163: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 163

2nd Grade ELA

The Little Prince

Chapter 13

Page 2

📖 Welcome to Day 163!

Today you will:

  • 📖 Read from The Little Prince
  • 📝 Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Compound Sentences
  • 🎯 Write: Opinion Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Root Words and Affixes

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Words

brave

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

📖 Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

📖 Reading: The Little Prince

Chapter 13

[Reading passage from The Little Prince, Chapter 13 will be inserted here from digital book database]

This section will include the actual chapter text with vocabulary words highlighted.

📚 Take your time reading! Think about what's happening in the story.

Page 5

🎯 Reading Comprehension

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 13]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 13]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about Chapter 13]

Page 6

✍️ Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 13]

Page 7

🎉

Great Job!

You've completed Day 163! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 163!

Day 164: 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📚

Day 164

2nd Grade ELA

  • 📰 Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • ✍️ Practice grammar: Action vs. Linking Verbs
  • 🎯 Write: Informative Writing
  • 🎨 Explore: Idioms and Figurative Language

You've completed Day 164! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 164!

Day 165: Day 165 Assessment - 2nd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

📝

Day 165

Assessment Day

The Little Prince

Page 2

📝 Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • 📚 Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 161-165
  • 🎯 Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Narrative Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

💡 Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

📚 Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. cottage

2. clever

3. forest

4. wicked

5. brave

6. kingdom

7. treasure

8. journey

9. adventure

10. magical

Page 4

🎯 Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

✏️ Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

📖 Writing Assessment

Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Requirements: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Page 7

🎉

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Little Prince.

See you on Day 166!

forest

Great Job!

You've completed Day 172! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

📖 What is it?
A prepositional phrase starts with a preposition (in, on, under, through, etc.) and ends with a noun or pronoun.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The house rose slowly through the air."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 10

💭 Opinion Writing

📝 Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Context Clues

📖 Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

🎯 Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 172!

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

📖 What is it?
A compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a comma and coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, so, for, yet, nor).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"The wind howled horribly, but Dorothy sat quite still."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

📖 Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

🎯 Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 173!

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

📖 What is it?
Action verbs show what someone does (run, jump, think). Linking verbs connect the subject to a description (is, was, seems, feels).

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Toto was (linking) a little black dog. Dorothy felt (linking) lonely. The wind howled (action) horribly."

✍️ Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 10

📖 Narrative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

💡 Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

📖 Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

🎯 Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 174!

📝

Day 175

  • ✏️ Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • ✍️ Writing: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

You've completed Day 176! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Little Prince.

Page 8

📰 Informational Text

Related Non-Fiction

Informational text content goes here.

📝 Comprehension Questions:

1. Question 1?

2. Question 2?

3. Question 3?

Page 9

✏️ Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

📖 What is it?
The past progressive tense shows an action that was ongoing in the past. Form it by using 'was' or 'were' + verb ending in '-ing'.

💡 Example from today's reading:
"Dorothy was walking (past progressive) when she saw (simple past) the Scarecrow."

✍️ Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 10

📝 Informative Writing

📝 Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

💡 Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

✅ Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 11

🎨 Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

📖 Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

🎯 Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

🌟

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Day 176!
